description: This satellite imagery map depicts a mountainous region, likely in Europe, showing two distinct Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) acquisition areas, "Burst A" (upper) and "Burst B" (lower), delineated by white rectangular outlines. The terrain features include vegetated mountain slopes, valleys, rocky peaks, and some urban development along a river in the eastern part of the image. Within Burst A, three points are labelled "v1", "v2", and "v3" across the western mountainous section. A white triangular marker with the label "P₀" is located in the eastern part of Burst A, situated within a valley containing visible settlements. The top right corner displays user interface elements consistent with Google Earth imagery, including a compass rose indicating North and a vertical zoom/scale bar. The image illustrates the spatial coverage and specific reference points relevant to interferometric processing of Sentinel-1 Terrain Observation with Progressive Scans (TOPS) data.
